summary: postinstall.js exhibits child_process + outbound HTTP pattern requiring human review
details: The package's postinstall.js contains a co-occurrence of require("child_process"), an http.request( call with a hostname literal, and GET method usage. This composition can indicate a postinstall dropper or exfiltration beacon, but can also reflect a legitimate prebuild/native-binary download pattern. The available evidence is keyword-level only (line numbers and tokens) without a traced execution path, so the destination, payload shape, and whether fetched bytes are executed cannot be confirmed from the provided signals. Routing for human review to inspect postinstall.js: verify the request hostname, whether the response is written to disk and executed (chmod+x / spawn / exec), and whether the destination is publisher-controlled and version-pinned.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
